metadata.name: Trample — excess damage tramples over to defending player
metadata.description: A creature with trample attacks and is blocked by a smaller creature. The attacker must assign lethal damage to the blocker, then the excess damage is assigned to the defending player. Tests CR 702.19: trample damage assignment.
metadata.generation_notes: CR 702.19a: If a creature with trample is blocked, the attacking player must assign at least lethal damage to all blockers. Any excess damage is assigned to the defending player. Informational script — documents the CR rule.

script.step_note: Darksteel Colossus (11/11 trample, indestructible) attacks. Solemn Simulacrum (2/2) blocks.

actions.description: Initial: Darksteel Colossus (11/11 trample) vs. Solemn Simulacrum (2/2), p2 at 40
Combat damage: 2 assigned to Solemn Simulacrum (lethal), 9 tramples to p2
actions.note: CR 702.19a: Attacker assigns 2 to Simulacrum (lethal damage for a 2-toughness creature), then 9 excess trample damage is assigned to p2. p2 goes from 40 to 31.
CR 704.5g: Solemn Simulacrum has 2 damage marked = lethal (toughness 2). Destroyed, goes to graveyard.
